response:
<<Length-Based Sort Query>>

To organize records by the character length of the `title` and `director` columns in descending order, while breaking ties by the actual text value, you can use the following SQL query. This approach ensures that if two entries have the same length, they are sorted alphabetically for consistent results:

```sql
SELECT title, director
FROM your_table_name
ORDER BY 
    LENGTH(title) DESC, 
    title ASC, 
    LENGTH(director) DESC, 
    director ASC;
```

*Note: Replace `your_table_name` with the actual name of your table. If you are using SQL Server, replace `LENGTH` with `LEN`.*
